a partial function is a binary relation over two sets that associates to every element of the first set at most one element of the second set

annotations.label: Square root as partial function on reals

anchor.snippet: because negative real numbers do not have real square roots, the operation can be viewed as a partial function
The domain of definition of a partial function is the subset S of X on which the partial function is defined
In case the domain of definition S is equal to the whole set X , the partial function is said to be total

A partial function is said to be injective , surjective , or bijective when the function given by the restriction of the partial function to its domain of definition is injective, surjective, or bijective, respectively
the term partial bijection denotes a partial function which is injective
An injective partial function may be inverted to an injective partial function
A partial transformation is a function

The natural logarithm function mapping the real numbers to themselves is a partial function

In denotational semantics a partial function is considered as returning the bottom element when it is undefined
In computer science a partial function corresponds to a subroutine that raises an exception or loops forever

The category of sets and partial bijections is equivalent to its dual
